content: It has a yellow fuel cap

Reference reply from 17: <think>The user is saying their car has a yellow fuel cap. This is actually a standard indicator for flex-fuel vehicles (FFV) in the US. Yellow gas cap = the vehicle can handle E85 (up to 85% ethanol). This is a common marking especially on Ford vehicles and other manufacturers that offer flex-fuel versions.
So this means their car IS flex-fuel capable. If that's the case, they can absolutely run E85 and can mix it with regular gasoline without issue, since the fuel system is designed for it.
